name: historical-channels
_comment: 湍河下游及白河汇流口的历史河道变迁 / Historical channel migration of the lower Tuan and its Bai-River confluence. Lower-reach change is driven by the Bai's documented avulsions at Gangtou (元大德七年 1304 夺潦河; 明隆庆四年 1570 徙西，遗老白河). Confluence position from 《清史稿·地理志》(湍水会白河于新野城东北). Han–Ming engineered redistribution (六门陂→朝水/刁河) from 《水经注》《晋书·杜预传》. Old-channel geometry is reconstructed (no OSM line exists); see `confidence`. Sources in each feature.

properties.name_zh: 古湍水下游·入淯水口（先秦—元，推定）
properties.description_zh: 湍水（古湍河）上中游受地形约束，自内乡经邓州城北折东南，与今大体一致；其下游入淯水（白河）之口在元代岗头夺潦河之前另在偏东处。此段为示意推定，仅供参考。
properties.description_en: The upper-middle Tuan, terrain-controlled, ran much as today (Neixiang → north of Dengzhou → SE). Before the Bai's 1304 capture of the Liao River at Gangtou, the Tuan met the Yu (Bai) further east. This reach is a schematic reconstruction only.

properties.name_zh: 汉—明·六门陂引湍入朝水（刁河）
properties.name_en: Han–Ming engineered diversion: Tuan → Chao (Diao) via the Six-Gate ponds
properties.description_zh: 人工“变道”：召信臣于前34年筑六门堰断湍水，下结二十九陂；晋杜预复六门陂，“诸陂散流，咸入朝水（今刁河）”。湍河之水经总干渠与陂塘网跨流域分入刁河，是历史上最大的人为水系改变，约明末淤废。
properties.description_en: An engineered avulsion: Shao Xinchen dammed the Tuan in 34 BCE (Six-Gate Weir) feeding 29 ponds; Du Yu (Jin) restored it so 'the ponds dispersed their flow, all entering the Chao (today's Diao)'. Tuan water was carried cross-basin into the Diao via the trunk canal and pond network — the largest historical man-made change to the drainage; silted up by the late Ming.

properties.description_zh: 元大德七年（1304）白河于岗头决口夺潦河，成今白河雏形；此后至明隆庆四年（1570）白河走偏东一线。1570年“清水由岗头徙而西”，此东侧河道遂废，新野东境留长约二十里（余）的故道，俗称老白河。湍河此期即汇入这条偏东的白河。
properties.description_en: After the Bai captured the Liao River at Gangtou in 1304, it ran along this eastern line until 1570, when 'the clear water shifted west from Gangtou', abandoning this channel — the ~20-li 'Old Bai' relict on Xinye's east. The Tuan joined this eastern Bai during 1304–1570.
properties.source: 新野县志；《清史稿·地理志》

properties.name_zh: 湍入白河·古汇流口（新野城东北，元—明）
properties.description_zh: 《清史稿·地理志》：白河“迳岗头镇……会湍水，合（新野）城东北，又西南，右纳刁河”。即此期湍河在新野城东北汇入（偏东的）白河，较今汇流口偏东北。
properties.description_en: Per the History of Qing: the Bai 'passes Gangtou … meets the Tuan northeast of [Xinye] city, then SW, taking in the Diao on the right.' The Tuan then joined the (eastern) Bai NE of Xinye — north-east of today's confluence.

properties.name_zh: 现行湍河入白河口（湍口村，明1570—今）
properties.description_zh: 1570年白河西徙后，湍河汇流口随之西移；今湍河经王集镇大韩营村、于新野城郊乡湍口村注入白河。湍河下游一段旧称“七里河”。
properties.description_en: After the Bai shifted west in 1570 the confluence moved west; today the Tuan passes Dahanying (Wangji) and empties into the Bai at Tuankou village (Chengjiao, Xinye). The lower Tuan was historically also called the 'Qili River'.
properties.source: 百度百科·湍河；新野县志

properties.name_zh: 岗头改道点（元1304夺潦河 · 明1570徙西）
properties.name_en: Gangtou avulsion node (1304 capture; 1570 westward shift)
properties.description_zh: 岗头（今潦河镇一带）是白河两次大改道的枢纽：元大德七年（1304）决口夺潦河形成今白河；明隆庆四年（1570）由岗头徙西，遗老白河。两次改道直接改变了湍河的入河口位置。
properties.description_en: Gangtou (near today's Liaohe town) was the hinge of the Bai's two great avulsions: the 1304 capture of the Liao River (forming the modern Bai) and the 1570 westward shift (leaving the Old Bai). Both moved the Tuan's mouth.
properties.source: 新野县志；《清史稿·地理志》
